zh.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton於1260年出生在英國英格蘭韋斯特 素可。她於1275年與Sir Henry FitzRogos在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾藍德瓦德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1350年在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾藍德瓦德享年90歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: English: plural form of   Fleming  .
Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleeman
meanings: English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.
English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: Cotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called from Old English (æt) cotum (dative plural of cot) ‘at the cottages or huts’ (or sometimes possibly from a Middle English plural, coten). Examples include Coton (Cambridgeshire, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire), Cottam (East Yorkshire, Lancashire, Nottinghamshire), and Cotham (Nottinghamshire). Particularly common in Staffordshire.
French: from a pet form of the personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ), derived via Nicot, or of the personal name Jacques , derived via Jacot.
History: John Cotton (1584–1652) was a noted Puritan preacher, who landed at Boston, MA, from London in 1633 and became leader of the Congregationalists in America.

variants.name: Cotten
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Cotton  .

Altered form of German Kotten, a habitational name from any of several places so named in Rhineland, Westphalia, Silesia, etc., or perhaps an American shortened form of composite German surnames such as Kottenhagen, Kottenhoff, Kottenkamp (see Koth ).

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Cotham
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Cotton  .

variants.name: Cottom
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Cotton  .

variants.name: Cottam
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): variant of   Cotton  .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
meanings: A name corrpted from Hannah, q.v. It is also found several times in a male name. From Ann, Anne, we have as nicknames Nan and Nancy

variants.name: Nancy
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nan
meanings: Originally a pet form of Ann (for the initial N-, compare Ned ). It is now also widely used as a short form of Nancy .

en.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton was born in 1260, in West Sussex, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir Henry FitzRogos in 1275, in Landwade, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1350, in Landwade, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton est née en 1260, à West Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir Henry FitzRogos en 1275, à Landwade, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1350, à Landwade, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 90 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton wurde 1260 in West Sussex, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1275 in Landwade, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Henry FitzRogos geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1350 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Landwade, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton è nata il nel 1260 nel West Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir Henry FitzRogos nel 1275, nel Landwade, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1350, nel Landwade, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 90 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton(은)는 1260년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 수세스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 랜드웨이드에서 Sir Henry FitzRogos(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 랜드웨이드에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Anne Fleming Cotton родилась в 1260 г. в местности вест суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла в 1350 г. в местности ландвад, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 90 лет.
